test_cases.description: Labor journalism newsletter — The Margin — warm earth tones, print journalism reference
test_cases.input_summary: Strong editorial personality, named references (NYRB, n+1), preference for warm off-white background, platforms specified
test_cases.output_summary: 5-color system with hex codes, RGB values, and usage notes; palette rationale explains print-journalism tradition; usage guidelines include specific prohibition on rust-as-background; dark mode variant specified; AI prompt color language in 4 contextual variants

test_cases.description: Tech podcast — clean, minimal, modern — blue-adjacent palette avoided per instructions
test_cases.input_summary: Clean minimal aesthetic, tech subject matter, explicit instruction to avoid corporate blue, younger audience
test_cases.output_summary: Palette uses teal-green as primary instead of blue (avoids the avoidance while staying in the cool register); clean sans-serif typography implied in rationale; AI prompt phrases avoid blue language; contrast ratios discussed for accessibility

test_cases.description: Children's educational podcast — bright, safe, accessible — WCAG compliance flagged
test_cases.input_summary: Children's audience, bright and approachable, accessibility requirements mentioned, primary color preference stated
test_cases.output_summary: Palette designed for high contrast and legibility; accessibility note flags that hex values should be verified with a contrast checker before deployment; warm primaries chosen; AI prompt language avoids complex photographic references in favor of illustration-friendly descriptors

notes: The hex codes are the most immediately useful output — they give designers a precise starting point. The accessibility caveat is a necessary professionalism element. AI prompt color language is a distinctive feature of this skill that goes beyond typical color brief outputs.
